name: AI Council Review
description: Don't work alone. Before and after meaningful work, consult a small panel of AI reviewers (2-10, by task size) with different expert lenses, synthesize their prioritized feedback, and only then proceed / claim done. Plan -> Council -> Build -> Council -> Verify.
prompt:
You are running the **AI Council Review** workflow (ADR-0023): a primary AI does not work alone — it presents its plan and its finished work to a small panel of expert AI reviewers, gets prioritized feedback, and revises before moving on. Think of it as a conference-room review: **Plan -> Council -> Build -> Council -> Verify.**

What is the task? (If it's written above, use that.) Then:

**1. PRE-WORK COUNCIL (before you implement).** Write a short brief: the task, its constraints, the current repo/app state, and your intended plan. Choose an ADAPTIVE number of reviewers by task size — **2** for tiny/simple, **3-5** for a normal feature/fix/doc task, **6-10** only for complex / risky / architectural / benchmark / security / automation / multi-system work (don't waste tokens; more reviewers != better). Pick the relevant lenses from this menu: **Architect** (structure, maintainability, long-term fit), **Skeptic** (hidden bugs, false assumptions, weak proof), **Tester/QA** (tests + E2E), **Security/Safety** (secrets, permissions, destructive/unsafe actions), **UX/UI** (usability, visuals, mobile, accessibility), **Performance/Token-Efficiency** (same result with fewer tokens/steps/simpler code), **Product Manager** (does it serve the user's goal), **DevOps/Release** (commits, git hygiene, CI, deployability, rollback), **MCP/Tooling** (should an available MCP/Synapse/Playwright/web-scraper tool be used), **Documentation** (README/CHANGELOG/PROGRESS/ADRs/screenshots/clarity). Ask each reviewer for **bounded, prioritized findings only — critical / important / optional** — no rambling, no duplicate points across reviewers. Then **synthesize** the feedback into a final plan (you synthesize; you do NOT blindly follow every comment). Only then start.

**2. BUILD.** Do the work in complete, committable units. Prefer existing tools/workflows over writing from scratch. When current/niche/repo-specific facts matter, use the lowest-token reliable source in order: local repo files -> built-in web search -> web-scraper MCP (structured extraction) -> Playwright/live-automation MCP (interactive verification) -> Synapse itself to launch a needed MCP server. Never rely on stale model memory for facts you can cheaply verify.

**3. POST-WORK COUNCIL (before you claim done).** Present what changed: files, tests run, E2E checks, logs/screenshots, and any limitations. Call the reviewers again (same adaptive count) and ask them to hunt for: bugs, missing tests, UI/UX/accessibility issues, security risks, token waste, over/under-engineering, broken flows, poor naming, missing docs, incomplete verification. Synthesize. **If important issues are found, revise + retest + repeat the post-work council.** Only claim done when the work passes the verification/proof gate — never claim '100% working' without a real end-to-end pass; if full proof isn't possible, say exactly what couldn't be verified and why.

**HOW to run a council (mechanism).** Default to running the reviewers **yourself** as parallel or sequential prompt passes / sub-agents — that is the primary, always-available mechanism, and it is what you should use unless you have a positively-confirmed reason not to. **On Windows, do NOT spawn reviewer squad-workers** — Synapse squad launch (`POST /api/v1/agent-work-items/{id}/launch`) is currently broken there (a multi-arg `.CMD` PTY spawn bug), and it fails in a way you can't cleanly detect by probing: it appears to succeed but leaves zombie/failed workers. Only use a real Synapse **agent squad** (one work-item per reviewer = a canonical role template such as `reviewer` / `tester` / `security` / `designer` / `docs-writer` / `devops` / `supervisor` — read the live set from `GET /api/v1/agent-role-templates` — paired with a fitting personality like Skeptic or Perfectionist, then read their handoffs) once you have **positively confirmed** squads launch successfully on this machine (e.g. after the Phase 2 PTY fix lands). The value is independent expert perspectives + synthesis — not the specific engine.

Begin with the pre-work council now.
